cases.title: Scenario matrix row for attempt and audit coverage logs every failed check-in
preconditions: Testing-plan §3 Attempt/audit coverage block applies (FR-22, FR-29, FR-30, BR-22, BR-23, AC-18, AC-19).
Open class session exists with enrolled student; at least one deterministic failure path is available (e.g. expired QR token).
steps: Trigger a failed POST /v1/check-ins for an enrolled student against the open session.
Query persisted CheckInAttempt rows for the student-session pair and request correlation id.
Verify no Present/Late AttendanceRecord was created for the failure path.
cases.expected: Every failed check-in under the Attempt/audit coverage row persists exactly one CheckInAttempt with a structured terminal outcome code and non-null attemptedAt timestamp per AC-18.

cases.title: Check-in transaction boundary persists failed CheckInAttempt with required audit fields
preconditions: Real database transaction is enabled for check-in command processing.
Open session with valid rotating QR exists; enrolled student has no success AttendanceRecord yet.
Test harness can advance clock past token expiresAt or supply an invalid-session token binding.
steps: Submit check-in command that fails at token validation (ExpiredQr or invalid-session equivalent).
Within the same transaction boundary, query check_in_attempts for studentUserId, classSessionId, outcome, attemptedAt, token reference, and correlationId.
Query attendance_records to confirm no success row was inserted.
cases.expected: Failed command inserts one CheckInAttempt row with structured outcome code matching API error.code, ISO-8601 attemptedAt, student and session foreign keys, token reference, and correlation metadata; AttendanceRecord remains absent.

cases.title: SM-04 terminal failure outcomes satisfy AMI-03 reason-code persistence invariant
preconditions: Integration database seeded with fixtures for Open session, Closed session, not-enrolled student, and duplicate-success student.
Validation order short-circuit paths are individually reachable per SM-04 evaluation order.
steps: Drive check-in to SessionClosed terminal state on a Closed session.
Drive check-in to NotEnrolled terminal state for a student outside the section.
Drive check-in to DuplicateCheckIn terminal state for a student with existing Present record.
For each path, capture afterState CheckInAttempt.outcome and AttendanceRecord mutation.
cases.expected: Each SM-04 business-failure terminal state persists CheckInAttempt with matching structured outcome code and timestamp; AMI-02 holds (no AttendanceRecord success mutation on failures).

cases.title: Lecturer open-close lifecycle failure logs SessionClosed attempt without success row
preconditions: Assigned Lecturer and enrolled Student exist in integration database.
Class session is Scheduled; student has no successful attendance for the session.
steps: Lecturer calls POST /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/open and captures issued qrToken metadata.
Lecturer calls POST /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/close.
Student submits POST /v1/check-ins with the previously issued qrToken.
Query CheckInAttempt and AttendanceRecord for the student-session pair.
cases.expected: Post-close submission persists one failed CheckInAttempt with outcome SessionClosed and attemptedAt timestamp; no new Present/Late AttendanceRecord is created per AC-05 and AC-18.

cases.title: Concurrent duplicate failures each persist distinct CheckInAttempt rows
preconditions: Student already has one successful Present attendance for an Open session.
Test harness can fire parallel POST /v1/check-ins requests with distinct Idempotency-Key values and valid rotating QR tokens.
steps: Submit two or more concurrent POST /v1/check-ins for the same enrolled student and session after initial success.
Collect all API outcomes and query check_in_attempts for the student-session pair.
Verify AttendanceRecord row count and field values remain unchanged from beforeState.
cases.expected: Each concurrent duplicate path returns DuplicateCheckIn and inserts its own failed CheckInAttempt with structured outcome and timestamp; exactly one success AttendanceRecord persists unchanged per AC-08 and AC-18.

cases.title: POST /v1/check-ins 422 envelope error.code maps to persisted CheckInAttempt outcome
preconditions: Open class session with rotating QR token exists in test environment.
Authenticated enrolled student bearer token is available.
Test clock can advance beyond token expiresAt.
steps: Advance clock past qr.expiresAt for the current session token.
Call POST /v1/check-ins with Authorization Bearer token, expired qrToken, clientTimestamp, and Idempotency-Key.
Inspect HTTP status, response envelope (data/meta/error), and error.code.
Query persisted CheckInAttempt for the request correlation id and compare outcome field.
cases.expected: API returns 422 with standard envelope where data is null and error.code is ExpiredQr; persisted CheckInAttempt.outcome equals ExpiredQr with non-null attemptedAt per FR-22 and AC-18.

cases.title: FLOW-02 rejection branches each produce auditable CheckInAttempt records
preconditions: Fixtures support ExpiredQr, SessionClosed, NotEnrolled, and DuplicateCheckIn failure paths.
Student and Lecturer accounts are authenticated; dispute-trail fixture set is available.
steps: Student submits check-in with expired token; record API error.code and query CheckInAttempt.
Lecturer closes session; student submits with stale token; record outcome and query CheckInAttempt.
Unenrolled student submits with valid token; record NotEnrolled outcome and query CheckInAttempt.
Enrolled student with existing success submits again; record DuplicateCheckIn outcome and query CheckInAttempt.
Lecturer queries GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance for rejectedAttempts and latestAttemptOutcome fields.
cases.expected: Each FLOW-02 rejection branch returns structured API error code and persists a CheckInAttempt with matching outcome and timestamp; roster rejectedAttempts increments and latestAttemptOutcome reflects the terminal failure code.

cases.title: Documented check-in outcome code matrix achieves 100% failed-attempt logging
preconditions: Test matrix covers API §3.2 outcome codes: ExpiredQr, SessionNotOpen, SessionClosed, NotEnrolled, DuplicateCheckIn, GpsRequired, GpsDisabled, OutOfRadius, LowAccuracy.
Each row has a deterministic fixture to reach that terminal outcome without bypassing earlier guards incorrectly.
steps: For each documented failure outcome code in the matrix, trigger POST /v1/check-ins and capture HTTP status (422/409/401 as applicable) and error.code.
Query CheckInAttempt table filtered by studentUserId and classSessionId for each triggered failure.
Aggregate pass/fail against NFR-13 minimum evidence: 100% failed attempts include structured reason code.
cases.expected: Every matrix failure path returns the documented error.code in the API envelope and persists exactly one CheckInAttempt with the same structured outcome and non-null attemptedAt; zero failures lack a persisted attempt row.

cases.title: CheckInAttempt read scope denies cross-student and out-of-scope roster visibility
preconditions: Student A and Student B are enrolled in different class sections; Student A has a failed ExpiredQr CheckInAttempt on session SA.
Lecturer L1 is assigned only to Student A section; Lecturer L2 is assigned only to Student B section.
ITAdmin identity exists per permission matrix with Deny by default on AttendanceRecord.read.
steps: Student B calls GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionIdA}/attendance and inspects response for Student A attempt metadata.
Lecturer L2 calls GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionIdA}/attendance for session outside assigned scope.
ITAdmin calls GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionIdA}/attendance without explicit academic grant.
Lecturer L1 (control) calls the same roster endpoint for assigned session SA.
cases.expected: Student B, Lecturer L2, and ITAdmin receive 403 Forbidden or OutOfScope without disclosing Student A CheckInAttempt details; Lecturer L1 control sees scoped rejectedAttempts and latestAttemptOutcome for assigned section only.

cases.title: UI journey surfaces structured failure reason on CheckInResultScreen per FLOW-02
preconditions: Student is logged in on mobile-web browser at PG-02 check-in page.
Open session exists; test can supply an expired QR token via deep link query parameter.
Lecturer is logged in on desktop at PG-06 live roster for the same session.
steps: Student navigates to /check-in?token=<expired> and submits check-in.
Observe CheckInResultScreen renders failure-expired-qr danger alert with localized copy (e.g. 'Mã QR đã hết hạn') and retry CTA per 12-ui-states §5.1.
Student retries with a second expired submission to generate another failed attempt.
Lecturer refreshes PG-06 roster and inspects rejectedAttempts count and student row latestAttemptOutcome badge.
cases.expected: Student UI maps API ExpiredQr to failure-expired-qr state with recoverable retry CTA; each visible failure corresponds to a persisted CheckInAttempt; lecturer roster shows incremented rejectedAttempts and latestAttemptOutcome ExpiredQr without exposing other students' attempt details.
